zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Cooper於1521年出生在英國艾可拉德文特時，她的父親Richard Cooper21歲，母親June Kingsmill22歲。她於1551年2月11日與Thomas Wale II在英國英格蘭艾可傑爾姆斯福德結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1604年10月31日在英國英格蘭哈普爾巴塞特享年83歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素瑞素瓦可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Cooper于1521年出生在英国艾可拉德文特时，她的父亲Richard Cooper21岁，母亲June Kingsmill22岁。她于1551年2月11日与Thomas Wale II在英国英格兰艾可傑爾姆斯福德结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1604年10月31日在英国英格兰哈普爾巴塞特享年83岁，埋葬在英国英格兰素瑞素瓦可。

nameMeanings.name: Cooper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker and repairer of wooden vessels such as barrels, tubs, buckets, casks, and vats, from Middle English couper, cowper (apparently from Middle Dutch kūper, a derivative of kūp ‘tub, container’, which was borrowed independently into English as coop). The prevalence of the surname, its cognates, and equivalents bears witness to the fact that this was one of the chief specialist trades in the Middle Ages throughout Europe. In North America, the English surname has absorbed some cases of like-sounding cognates from other languages, for example Dutch Kuiper .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Kupfer and Kupper (see Kuper ).
Dutch: occupational name for a buyer or merchant, Middle Dutch coper.

variants.name: Kuiper
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Hendrik, Willem, Harm, Klaas, Kort, Marinus, Nicolaas, Wouter.
Dutch: occupational name for a cooper, Middle Dutch cup(e)re.

variants.name: Couper
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Cooper  .

variants.name: Hooper
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name for a cooper, someone who fitted wooden or metal hoops on wooden casks and barrels, or a barrel-maker, from Middle English hoper, an agent derivative of hop ‘hoop, band’. Compare Cooper .

variants.name: Coop
meanings: English (Lancashire): metonymic occupational name for a cooper, from Middle English coupe, cupe ‘tub, cask’ (from Latin cūpa), probably used for a cooper; compare Cooper . In some cases the surname may have been derived from a pub or house sign.
Americanized form of German or Dutch   Koop  .
History: It was David Koop (his father's surname also spelled Kupe) from Germany whose surname was changed to Coop after he settled in PA toward the end of the 18th century. He later moved to VA.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Cooper was born in 1521, in Radwinter, Essex, England, her father, Richard Cooper, was 21 and her mother, June Kingsmill, was 22. She married Thomas Wale II on 11 February 1551, in Chelmsford, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died on 31 October 1604, in Bassett, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 83, and was buried in Southwark, Surrey, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Cooper est née en 1521, à Radwinter, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Richard Cooper, avait 21 ans et sa mère, June Kingsmill, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Wale II le 11 février 1551, à Chelmsford, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 31 octobre 1604, à Bassett, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 83 ans, et a été enterrée à Southwark, Surrey, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Cooper 1521 in Radwinter, Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Cooper 21 und ihre Mutter June Kingsmill 22. Sie hat am 11. Februar 1551 in Chelmsford, Essex, England, United Kingdom Thomas Wale II geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 31. Oktober 1604 im Alter von 83 Jahren in Bassett, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Southwark, Surrey, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Cooper è nata il nel 1521 nel Radwinter, Essex, England, sua padre, Richard Cooper, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, June Kingsmill, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Wale II il 11 febbraio 1551, nel Chelmsford, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 31 ottobre 1604, nel Bassett, Hampshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 83 anni, e fu sepolta a Southwark, Surrey, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Cooperが1521年にイギリスエセックスラドウィンターで生まれた時，父Richard Cooperは21歳，母June Kingsmillは22歳であった。1551年2月11日にイギリスイギリスエセックスチェルムスフォードでThomas Wale IIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1604年10月31日にイギリスイギリスハンプシュアイアバセットで83歳で死去し，イギリスイギリススリーソウスワークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Cooper(이)가 1521년 영국 에세스 라드윈터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Cooper(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 June Kingsmill(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1551년 2월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 헬스퍼드에서 Thomas Wale II(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1604년 10월 31일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 배싯에서 83세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수리 소우스워크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Cooper nasceu em 1521, em Radwinter, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Cooper, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, June Kingsmill, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Wale II em 11 de fevereiro de 1551, em Chelmsford, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 31 de outubro de 1604, em Bassett, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 83 anos, e foi sepultada em Southwark, Surrey, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Cooper родилась в 1521 г. в местности радвинтир, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Cooper было 21 год, а матери, которую звали June Kingsmill, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Wale II 11 февраля 1551 г. в местности челмсфорд, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла 31 октября 1604 г. в местности бассетт, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 83 лет и была похоронена в местности соутварк, сурри, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Cooper nació en 1521, en Radwinter, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Cooper, tenía 21 años y su mamá, June Kingsmill, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Thomas Wale II el 11 de febrero de 1551, en Chelmsford, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 31 de octubre de 1604, en Bassett, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 83 años, y fue sepultada en Southwark, Surrey, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
